cta_status.criterion_3_weakness: Question does not embed the specific claim (122% / test fixture) directly -- description adds context but the spoken and visual lines do not

hook_breakdown.visual_gap_location: scene-marker t=1.7s (subline entrance) to t=8.5s (marker redraw)

findings.summary: Hook scene has a 6.8s dead gap (t=1.7s to t=8.5s) with no qualifying new visual content -- directly causes the too-much-talking-without-screen-changes problem the user flagged
findings.evidence: scene-marker.html GSAP timeline: pretitle t=0.0s, headline t=0.3s, marker sweep t=1.1s, subline entrance t=1.7s. Next qualifying beat: marker redraw at t=8.5s. Gap = 6.8s. Mid-beats at t=4.5s (opacity yoyo) and t=6.5s (scale yoyo) do NOT qualify per visual-pacing-5s.md -- scale yoyo and opacity pulse on existing elements do not advance the viewer information state. In those 6.8s the narration delivers 5 punchy phrases: 'Right now.' (3.14s), 'It just flagged...' (3.88s), 'In quotes.' (6.72s), 'Inside a test fixture.' (7.62s), 'Then it billed you to investigate.' (9.20s). Each is a narration beat the viewer hears but sees nothing new on screen.
findings.fix: Add 2-3 new visual entrances paced to transcript word anchors. Suggested: (1) At t=3.14s ('Right now'): reveal a small mono-pill badge showing '164 grep hits / 22 files' -- new information, tiny footprint. (2) At t=6.72s ('In quotes'): reveal a monospace code snippet card showing `script: console.log("hi")` from dag-executor.test.ts -- the actual false-positive receipt. 48px monospace, max-width 860px. tl.from() at 6.72s. (3) Optionally at t=4.8s: a '22 files vs 12 files' counter chip bridging the gap. Each is a real content beat, not an animation flourish, and each maps directly to a narration phrase.

findings.summary: The 'cent per refactor' token-pricing estimate and '$15/dev/month' total are not explicitly sourced in any research artifact
findings.evidence: Script: '22 files of false positives is roughly 11 thousand tokens. Call it a cent per refactor. Scale it up. Fifty refactors a day, 30 days a month -- that is 15 dollars per developer.' The 11K token estimate IS sourced (content-brief.md section 5: 22 Read calls x ~500 tokens = ~11K). The cent per refactor pricing is an unattributed assumption. content-brief.md section 5 gives token counts only, no dollar conversion. At Claude Haiku input pricing ($0.80/1M tokens): 11K x $0.80/1M = $0.0088 per refactor (close to 1 cent). At Sonnet: $0.033. The brief does not specify a model tier for the estimate.
findings.fix: Option A: attribute the model tier -- 'Call it a cent per refactor at Haiku-tier pricing.' Option B (safer): drop the dollar conversion entirely and keep only the token receipt -- '22 files of false positives is roughly 11 thousand tokens of wasted file context. Per refactor. Pure waste.' The 122% over-count stat already carries the argument; the $15 figure adds rhetorical weight but is an unattributed back-of-envelope calculation.

findings.summary: CTA criterion 3 (references a specific claim) is weak -- the spoken and visual questions do not embed the key stat (122%) and 'one config line' is factually imprecise (the install is two commands)
findings.evidence: Spoken: 'Are you fixing that today with one config line? Or accepting the waste?' Visual (cta-question): 'One config line -- or accept the waste?' Description: 'Are you fixing that today with one config line, or accepting the waste?' None embeds the receipt (122%, useState, console.log) directly. The preceding narration sentence does: 'Your AI agent burns money on every refactor. 122 percent more work than it needs.' Criterion 3 is arguably met by proximity but the engagement-cta.md examples show the specific claim inside the question text, not before it. Additionally 'one config line' is imprecise: the install scene (script line 13) shows TWO commands (NPM install + NPX skills add), making the CTA anchor factually misleading.
findings.fix: Rewrite the spoken closer to embed the claim and fix the factual anchor: 'Your agent over-counted by 122 percent today. Are you adding ast-grep with two commands right now -- or staying on plain text search?' Tighten to 14-16 words for Short budget. Update #cta-question in scene-cta.html and the description engagement line to match.
